subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing are presented with clear tables, headings, units and uncertainties.
Dual approaches to uncertainty (range and propagation) are applied correctly and documented thoroughly.
Propagation of uncertainty formulas and calculations for wire radius are well explained and systematic.
weaknesses: Some rounding and formatting inconsistencies (e.g. mis-aligned symbols, unexplained rounding of uncertainties) slightly reduce precision.
Gradient values lack confidence intervals or RÂ² statistics, limiting the evaluation of fit quality.
Variable notation fluctuates (uppercase vs lowercase), and axis labels/figure references are occasionally missing.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strandsâcommunication of data processing, consideration of uncertainties, and quality of data processingâsit firmly in the top band (5/6). Minor inconsistencies in formatting, rounding and statistical reporting prevent a perfect 6, so a score of 5 reflects very strong performance with small but notable areas to refine.

strengths: Context is clearly tied to Ohmâs law with citation of a literature value for nichrome resistivity, grounding the investigation in accepted data.
Step-by-step methodology with photographs, diagrams and a detailed materials list with ranges and resolutions enables reproducibility.
Explicit repetition of trials and systematic variation of cross-sectional area minimizes ambiguity in the experimental procedure.
weaknesses: Research question wording is verbose and could be streamlined; industrial relevance of varying surface area is not fully justified.
Alligator-clip spacing and room conditions are not specified, introducing minor uncertainties in reproducibility.
Unit conversions between mmÂ² and mÂ² are inconsistent and lack explicit conversion steps, risking ambiguity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student achieves the top band in question context (5/6) and in methodological description for reproducibility (5/6), with only minor omissions preventing a perfect 6. Methodological considerations of controls are well discussed but lack full explanation of âsufficientâ data sampling, placing this strand at the upper end of the 3â4 band. Overall, a score of 5 accurately reflects high performance with minor areas for improvement.

strengths: Conclusion states the numerical resistivity with uncertainty and links the trend to the theoretical Râ1/A relationship.
Numerical agreement with theory is clearly articulated and supported by the analysis.
weaknesses: Comparison to the accepted literature value is brief, with limited discussion of additional systematic errors beyond contact resistance.
Scientific context comparison lacks depthâother sources of discrepancy (e.g., temperature drift, wire variability) are not quantified.
Wording errors and omission of figure references reduce clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis and justifies the result (band 5), but the comparison to scientific context is only partially developed (band 4). Balancing a strong data-driven conclusion against a superficial context discussion yields an overall score of 4.

strengths: Identifies multiple specific methodological weaknesses (e.g., meter precision, contact resistance, temperature drift) and links each to systematic or random error.
Proposes relevant, realistic improvements (e.g., custom clip jig, higher-precision meter) with clear mitigation rationale.
Explores an extension to study temperature effects, demonstrating thoughtful consideration of further investigation.
weaknesses: Improvement suggestions lack quantitative estimates of how much each change would reduce overall uncertainty.
Discussion of the relative impact of each improvement is concise but could be expanded for full depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both evaluation strandsâmethodological weaknesses and suggested improvementsâdemonstrate explanation of impact and relevance, placing them in the top band. Minor brevity in quantitative estimation prevents a full 6/6, so a score of 5 best reflects comprehensive, thoughtful evaluation with room for deeper quantification.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student situates the question within the very specific context of determining the resistivity of nichrome by systematically altering total cross-sectional area through parallel wiring, and links the idea to Ohmâs law, resistivity, and a published reference value.  The justification of why surface area is a relevant variable and how it relates to resistivity indicates a clear and appropriate context; only minor depth could be added (e.g., industrial relevance), so the low end of the top band is suitable.

Instrument resolution is recorded, absolute and percentage uncertainties are calculated for every derived quantity, error bars plotted, and both range and propagation methods used.  Some propagation algebra is imprecise, but consideration is still appropriate and systematic, meriting the lower half of the top band.

Controls (length, temperature, purity) are identified and their importance discussed; a variable table links each to potential error, demonstrating relevance.  However, justification of why three trials are sufficient, or why ten area increments ensure âsufficientâ data, is only stated, not fully explained, so the strand fits the upper end of the 3â4 band.

Five concrete weaknesses (contact, meter precision, temperature, isolation, clip quality) are listed, each linked to the specific type of error (systematic/random) and its likely magnitude or direction, demonstrating explanation of relative impact; hence 5 in the top band.

Step-by-step instructions, photographs/diagrams, a full materials table with ranges/resolutions, and explicit repetition (three trials for ten levels) allow another investigator to reproduce the work with little ambiguity.  Minor omissions (exact clip spacing, exact room conditions) prevent a perfect 6, but clarity is strong enough for the top band.

Averages, hyperbolic fits, linearisation, gradient extraction and resistivity calculation are all shown; values are internally consistent and carried through with uncertainty.  Minor rounding inconsistencies occur, so 5/6 is justified.

For every weakness a realistic improvement is proposed and the way it mitigates error is clarified (e.g., custom jig to ensure uniform contact, higher-precision meter to reduce uncertainty).  Explanations align directly with identified limitations, satisfying the descriptor for the 5â6 band; minor brevity justifies a 5.

Raw and processed data are tabulated with clear headings, units and uncertainties; significant figures are generally consistent and graphs are properly titled.  A few formatting glitches (mis-aligned symbols) limit precision slightly, so 5ânot 6âis awarded.

A literature resistivity is cited and percentage error computed, showing relevant comparison.  Discussion of why the discrepancy exists is limited to a brief mention of contact resistance; nonetheless the comparison is present and partially justified, fitting 3â4 band with the higher score.

The student states numerical resistivity with uncertainty, describes the observed hyperbolic trend, and explicitly links both to the theoretical relationship Râ1/A.  The conclusion is fully consistent with processed data; small wording errors lower confidence slightly, so 5 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: Gradient values are given without confidence intervals or RÂ². Including these statistics would allow evaluation of fit quality and strengthen analysis.

The research question is verbose and could be streamlined to clearly state the independent and dependent variables. Consider specifying why increasing surface area via parallel wires is industrially relevant to add depth.
The background theory provides a clear explanation of metallic bonding and resistance at constant temperature, effectively linking atomic structure to macroscopic conductivity.
The parallel resistance equation is shown, but you could expand it to include resistivity explicitly (e.g. $$R=\rho L/A$$) and clarify each symbol for reader clarity.
Citing a literature value for nichrome resistivity at 20 Â°C grounds the investigation in accepted data and strengthens your contextual justification.
The formulas and predicted trend table is informative but inconsistent citation formatting is distracting. Standardize references and align cell contents for readability.
The hypothesis clearly predicts a hyperbolic decrease in resistance, but you could include an explicit model (e.g. $$R=\frac{\rho L}{nA_\text{single}}$$) to strengthen quantitative expectations.
Variable table mixes mmÂ² and mÂ² units without clear conversion steps. Ensure cross-sectional area units are consistent (mÂ²) and document conversion math explicitly.
The description of how resistivity is calculated mentions mmÂ² instead of mÂ², creating ambiguity. Correct the unit notation to maintain reproducibility.
Method step lacks specification of alligator-clip spacing. Adding exact clip positions would reduce systematic uncertainty and support reproducibility.
Materials table effectively lists ranges and resolutions, aiding uncertainty analysis and replicability.
Raw data table is well presented with uncertainties and clear headings, facilitating precise data recording assessment.
Example calculation uses proper averaging formula but set notation is confusing. Simplify to $$R_{avg}=(R_1+R_2+R_3)/3$$ for clarity.
Range-method uncertainty is calculated correctly and shows systematic application of error analysis.
Uncertainty in cross-sectional area is rounded from 0.628â0.6 without justification. Retain significant figures or explain rounding criteria.
Propagation of uncertainty for wire radius is applied correctly and the formula is well documented.
Convert diameter units at the outset (mmâm) in a dedicated step to prevent confusion in subsequent radius and area calculations.
Variable notation for resistivity calculation fluctuates between lowercase and uppercase. Use consistent symbol case (e.g. $$\rho$$) throughout.
Table of calculated resistivities could include propagated uncertainty for every entry and RÂ² of linear fits to strengthen data processing quality.
Dual uncertainty methods (propagation and range) are presented side-by-side, demonstrating comprehensive consideration of measurement errors.
Graphical method description omits mention of figure number and axis labels; add clear figure references and include units on axes to improve precision.
The conclusion cogently links theoretical trend to experimental data and restates the resistivity result with uncertainty.
Consider discussing additional systematic errors such as wire diameter variability or temperature drift and quantify their potential impact on resistivity.
Improvement plans are realistic but could be strengthened by estimating how much each change would reduce the overall uncertainty quantitatively.
The proposed extension to study temperature effects uses the same apparatus effectively and demonstrates thoughtful exploration of a new variable.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
